transcript:
HCP: hello, come on in and have a seat, i'm dr cheng, can you tell me what brings you here today
Patient: yeah sure, it's just the anxiety again, it's been getting worse lately
HCP: okay, can you describe what that anxiety feels like for you
Patient: it's like a tight chest, i can't stop checking the locks on my front door, maybe ten times a day
HCP: and how is that affecting your sleep or daily life
Patient: i can't concentrate at work, i keep making mistakes, and i haven't slept in three nights because i'm just too worried
HCP: i notice you're speaking quite fast and looking around a lot, is that how you feel today
Patient: yeah i feel on edge, like i have to do something right now or something bad will happen
HCP: do you feel guilty about this or think you're doing something wrong
Patient: no not guilty, just scared, but i know i should calm down, it's just the panic again

behaviour.evidence_span: i can't stop checking the locks on my front door

speech.evidence_span: i notice you're speaking quite fast

mood.evidence_span: it's just the anxiety again, it's been getting worse lately

thought.evidence_span: like i have to do something right now or something bad will happen

cognition.evidence_span: i can't concentrate at work, i keep making mistakes, and i haven't slept in three nights

insight.text: partial insight — recognizes symptoms as panic
insight.evidence_span: but i know i should calm down, it's just the panic again

transcript:
HCP: hello, thanks for seeing me today
Patient: yeah, hi doctor
HCP: so what brings you in?
Patient: anxiety attacks, been happening a lot lately
HCP: can you describe one for me
Patient: my heart races, hands shake, can't breathe properly
HCP: and when do these happen
Patient: usually when i have to go somewhere public
HCP: how is your mood today
Patient: okay, just a bit jumpy
HCP: do you feel guilty about this
Patient: no not really
HCP: do you think you have a problem
Patient: yeah i know something is wrong with me

thought.evidence_span: my heart races, hands shake, can't breathe properly

insight.evidence_span: yeah i know something is wrong with me

transcript:
HCP: hello, thanks for coming in, how are you going today
Patient: alright i guess, just a bit overwhelmed
HCP: can you tell me what's been bothering you lately
Patient: yeah, i've been feeling really anxious about work, can't stop worrying about emails
HCP: and how does that anxiety affect your sleep
Patient: i find myself awake for hours, just replaying every mistake i made
HCP: you sound quite stressed, is your heart racing or feeling shaky too
Patient: yeah, my hands are trembling and i can't focus on anything
HCP: have you noticed any changes in your appetite or energy levels
Patient: not really, just feel drained all the time
HCP: do you think you need some support for this
Patient: i know i should see someone, but i'm not sure if i'll get better

thought.evidence_span: just replaying every mistake i made

insight.text: partial insight — acknowledges need for help but doubtful of outcome
insight.evidence_span: i know i should see someone, but i'm not sure if i'll get better

transcript:
HCP: hello, i'm dr lee, how are you doing today
Patient: hi dr lee, yeah i'm okay i suppose, just really tired all the time
HCP: sounds like you've had a rough few weeks, what brings you in today
Patient: well mostly the anxiety, i feel like i can't breathe when i'm in public
HCP: that sounds really frightening, how long has this been going on
Patient: probably about three months now, since my mum got sick, i just get panicky
HCP: and has this affected your daily life at work or home
Patient: yes, i can't leave the house much, i'm avoiding the shops and the bus stop
HCP: i understand that's hard, have you noticed any changes in your sleep or appetite
Patient: not really, i eat okay but i'm definitely not sleeping properly, waking up at 3am
HCP: ok, and do you feel this is related to your mother's condition
Patient: yeah i think so, i'm just scared something bad will happen to her
HCP: do you feel you're losing control over these thoughts
Patient: sometimes i feel like i can't stop worrying, it just takes over my mind
HCP: and do you have any idea what treatment might help you
Patient: i guess maybe some medication or therapy, i'm not sure what you want me to do though
HCP: we'll work that out together, for now tell me about your concentration
Patient: it's pretty bad, i can't focus on anything, just distracted by the worry

behaviour.evidence_span: i can't leave the house much, i'm avoiding the shops and the bus stop

mood.evidence_span: i feel like i can't breathe when i'm in public

thought.text: excessive worry, fear of harm to mother
thought.evidence_span: i'm just scared something bad will happen to her

cognition.evidence_span: i'm definitely not sleeping properly, waking up at 3am

insight.evidence_span: i'm not sure what you want me to do though

transcript:

transcript:
HCP: hi there, thanks for seeing me today, how are you going
Patient: okay i suppose, just a bit on edge
HCP: can you tell me what brings you in
Patient: i've been having these loud voices again, telling me i'm a failure
HCP: when do you hear them
Patient: mostly in the morning, they're so clear and loud
HCP: and what do they say to you
Patient: they say everyone hates me and i should give up
HCP: are you hearing anyone else say anything
Patient: no just the one voice, it's really annoying
HCP: have you noticed any changes in your sleep or appetite
Patient: i'm eating less and staying up late because the voices keep me awake
HCP: do you feel your thinking is clear today
Patient: not really, i feel foggy and confused
HCP: do you understand why you are here
Patient: yeah, to get the voices stopped, but i don't know if meds will work

thought.evidence_span: having these loud voices again, telling me i'm a failure

insight.text: partial insight — accepts need for help but skeptical about efficacy
insight.evidence_span: to get the voices stopped, but i don't know if meds will work

transcript:
HCP: hi there, thanks for meeting me today, how's it going
Patient: yeah, okay i guess, just tired honestly
HCP: can you tell me what brings you here
Patient: mostly just anxiety, feels like my heart is pounding all the time
HCP: where does that feeling come from usually
Patient: mostly when i have to do stuff at work or talk to people
HCP: how are you sleeping lately
Patient: terrible, i wake up at 3am and just stare at the ceiling
HCP: do you feel worried about things happening to you
Patient: yes, like something bad is going to happen anytime soon
HCP: that sounds really distressing, do you feel safe
Patient: mostly safe but i'm scared i'll panic and pass out
HCP: have you noticed your memory changing
Patient: yeah, i forget what i was saying mid-sentence a lot
HCP: and how do you view your condition now
Patient: i know i'm anxious but i don't think it's an illness

mood.evidence_span: that sounds really distressing, do you feel safe

thought.evidence_span: like something bad is going to happen anytime soon

cognition.evidence_span: i wake up at 3am and just stare at the ceiling

insight.text: partial insight - acknowledges anxiety but denies illness

transcript:
HCP: hello, good to see you, what brings you in today
Patient: i've been having these loud ringing noises in my ears again, it's getting worse
HCP: how long has the ringing been happening
Patient: about three weeks, started after that loud concert last month
HCP: and are you finding it hard to focus on work
Patient: yeah, i can't concentrate, my brain feels foggy and slow
HCP: you mentioned the noise, does that make you feel anxious
Patient: yes, i get really panicked when the ringing gets loud, heart races fast
HCP: how is your mood managing with all this stress
Patient: i'm irritable and angry, snapping at my partner all the time

behaviour.evidence_span: snapping at my partner all the time

cognition.evidence_span: i can't concentrate, my brain feels foggy and slow

transcript:
HCP: hi, thanks for coming in today, how are you feeling
Patient: honestly, not great, i feel really anxious and on edge
HCP: can you tell me more about that anxiety
Patient: it's constant, i can't sit still, i keep checking my phone every few minutes
HCP: and how has your mood been lately
Patient: irritable, short-tempered, just want everyone to leave me alone
HCP: do you notice your heart racing or sweating a lot
Patient: yeah all the time, feels like something bad is going to happen
HCP: you seem quite restless in here, is that how you feel too
Patient: yeah i pace around, i can't just sit in one spot
HCP: do you understand why you're here today
Patient: i know i need help, i just don't know if anything will work
